informal_statement.text: Given a sorted slice of integers in nondecreasing order and a target value, return Some(i) where arr[i] == target, or None if the target is absent. If multiple indices match, returning any valid index is acceptable.
preconditions: arr is sorted in nondecreasing order
required_properties: if the function returns Some(i), then i < arr.len() and arr[i] == target
if the function returns None, then target does not occur in arr
the function terminates for every input
edge_cases: single-element slice not containing the target

semantic_units.description: Input precondition: the slice is sorted in nondecreasing order.
common_failure_modes: asserting strict ordering instead of nondecreasing

semantic_units.description: Success postcondition: if the return is Some(i), then i is a valid index and arr[i] == target.
Failure postcondition: if the return is None, then target does not occur in arr.
common_failure_modes: only quantifying over a prefix or suffix
stating the weaker claim that target is not at a specific position

semantic_units.description: Termination: the function terminates on every input (lo..hi shrinks each iteration).
Uniqueness is NOT required: multiple valid indices exist when duplicates are present.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (t : Int) (i : Nat), binarySearch arr t = some i → i < arr.length ∧ arr.get? i = some t
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the function returns Some i, then i is in range and arr[i] equals the target.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (t : Int), SortedLE arr → binarySearch arr t = none → ∀ i, i < arr.length → arr.get? i ≠ some t
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: Assuming the input is sorted in nondecreasing order, if the function returns None then the target does not occur anywhere in the slice.

generated_obligations.nl_gloss: No additional global preconditions beyond having an array and a target; the key assumptions are stated in later obligations.
The input slice/array is sorted in nondecreasing order (i.e., for all i<j, arr[i] ≤ arr[j]).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), let r := rust_reference arr target; r = Option Nat ∧ (r = none ↔ ¬ (∃ i : Nat, i < arr.size ∧ arr[i] = target))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The result is an Option index; it is None exactly when the target does not occur anywhere in the array.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), let r := rust_reference arr target; (∃ i : Nat, i < arr.size ∧ arr[i] = target) → (∃ k : Nat, k < arr.size ∧ rust_reference arr target = some k ∧ arr[k] = target)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the target occurs in the array, the implementation returns Some(k) for some valid index k where arr[k] = target.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), let r := rust_reference arr target; r = some k → k < arr.size
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Any returned index is within bounds of the array.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), let r := rust_reference arr target; r = some k → arr[k] = target
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the implementation returns Some(k), then the element at that index equals the target.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), let r := rust_reference arr target; r = none → ¬ (∃ i : Nat, i < arr.size ∧ arr[i] = target)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the implementation returns None, then there is no index containing the target.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: For the internal loop/recursion of rust_reference, there exists a natural-number variant that strictly decreases on each iteration and is bounded below by 0
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The algorithm terminates (e.g., binary search loop decreases the search interval size).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: For the internal loop/recursion with search bounds lo hi, maintain: (∀ i : Nat, i < arr.size → (i < lo ∨ i ≥ hi) → arr[i] ≠ target) ∧ lo ≤ hi
For the internal loop/recursion, maintain that the target can only be in the current interval: (∃ i : Nat, i < arr.size ∧ arr[i] = target) → (lo ≤ i ∧ i < hi)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Loop invariant: if the target exists, then it lies within the current search interval.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: Assuming SortedNondecreasing arr and the loop invariant, after choosing mid and updating bounds based on comparison with target, the invariant is preserved
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The binary-search step using sortedness preserves the invariant and shrinks the interval correctly.
